videos.title: Chain of Thought: Introducing Audio MultiChallenge
videos.description:
Brad Kenstler, Head of Agent Capabilities and Environments, discusses our first benchmark focused on audio with Advait Gosai, Research Engineer, Brian Jang, AI Product Manager, and Tyler Vuong, Research Scientist. 

0:00 Overview of Audio MultiChallenge
3:48 Why are audio models different?
8:20 Audio vs Text MultiChallenge
12:06 Challenges on data and multi-turn
21:10 Benchmark design
25:27 Results
28:50 Rubric and Judge design
31:55 Where audio models struggle
38:30 S2S vs ASR TTS
45:20 Implications on Scaling


Check out the Leaderboard here: https://scale.com/leaderboard/audiomc

videos.title: Fireside Chat: AI Reimagining Qatar's Cultural Experience
videos.description:
AI is shaping the future of culture and tourism in Qatar.

Watch the full webinar with Qatar Museums and Scale AI on how AI enables personalized, human-centered cultural experiences.

The fireside chat is featuring:

Asma Al-Jefairi: Director of Digital Experience @ Qatar Museums
Norah Abokhodair: Head of Upskilling @ Scale AI
Salma Soliman: AI Product Manager @ Scale AI
Ayman Adil: Applied AI Engineer @ Scale AI

videos.title: Chain of Thought: Introducing ResearchRubrics
videos.description:
Brad Kenstler, Head of Agent Capabilities and Environments, talk about ResearchRubrics with Calvin Zhang, ML Research Operations Lead, and Manasi Sharma, Machine Learning Research Engineer. 

0:00 Introduction
4:29 Overview of deep research agents
9:00 Complexity framework for the benchmark
15:38 Why use human rubrics?
22:38 Rubric design
28:41 Rubric categories
32:59 Ensuring reliable evaluation
35:45 Paper findings 
38:45 Agents evaluated and insights
41:53 Surprising results
46:07 Insights for training research agents
52:05 Frontier for deep research agents

Read the paper here: https://scale.com/research/researchrubrics

videos.title: Chain of Thought: MoRe Bench
videos.description:
Introducing MoRe Bench, our benchmark on moral reasoning. MoRe Bench evaluates the ability to reason on moral decisions as well as making moral decisions autonomously.
Learn more here: https://scale.com/research/morebench

0:00 Introductions
2:00 Benchmark overview
8:07 Motivation behind benchmark
11:06 Why moral reasoning matters
17:25 Benchmark scenarios and tasks
24:15 Rubrics for More Bench
30:38 Rubric design
40:20 Evaluation design
56:30 Metrics deep dive
1:06:02 Explaining results
1:13:32 On moral theory
1:20:06 RLHF for moral reasoning
1:26:50 Future areas of interest

videos.title: Diving into Enterprise Healthcare AI for 2026
videos.description:
The team is kicking off 2026 like the rest of us--by focusing on health(care)! They discuss why adopting AI in healthcare is different from other enterprise AI initiatives and how leaders can account for those differences. And as always, they react to some of the internet's hottest takes on AI (healthcare edition!).

Welcome to Human in the Loop.
Scale is the humanity-first AI company. We believe the most powerful and responsible AI systems are built with humans in the loop. And now, it’s also the inspiration behind our new video podcast series Human in the Loop: your guide to understanding what it takes to build and deploy real-world enterprise AI systems.

We share our learnings from working with the largest enterprises, foundation model builders, and governments to equip you with the insights you need to build practical, powerful AI systems in your organization. 

Learn more: scale.com/enterprise/agentic-solutions

videos.title: Chain of Thought: Introducing Remote Labor Index (RLI)
videos.description:
Introducing the Remote Labor Index, RLI. Brad Kenstler, Head of Agent Capabilities and Environments, discusses RLI with Bing Liu, Head of Research, Madhu Sehwag, Research Scientist, and Mantas Mazeika, Research Scientist at the Center for AI Safety. 

The Remote Labor Index (RLI) is a benchmark that empirically measures the capability of AI agents to perform real-world, economically valuable remote work.

0:00 Introduction
1:00 Overview of RLI
5:11 Benchmarking Freelance work
10:32 Comparing RLI to other professional domain benchmarks
12:18 Deep dive on RLI tasks
17:27 Making tasks representative of real-world work
22:50 Rubrics vs judge-based evaluation
26:15 Bottlenecks on agentic capabilities
29:30 Which agents does RLI evaluate
34:04 Failure modes of RLI
37:30 Implications on the future of remote labor
42:10 Unlocking performance improvements on RLI

Learn more about the benchmark at: https://scale.com/leaderboard/rli

videos.title: We predicted the future of AI in 2025…were we right? plus our 2026 predictions
videos.description:
Today on the podcast, the Enterprise team reviews AI predictions they made 6 months ago and sees if they came true or not. Spoiler: we got a lot wrong...but some unexpected things right!

They conclude by predicting what we can expect to see in 2026 with enterprise AI.

Watch the original 2025 AI predictions video here: https://youtu.be/I_GBaiK5W-Y 

00:00 Introductions
01:00 Reviewing 2025 AI Predictions
26:16 Making Predictions for 2026

videos.title: Introducing Scale Robotics Lab
videos.description:
Scale is solving the bottleneck holding back Physical AI. By building the Data Engine, we are driving the next generation of embodied intelligence. We introduce here our approach to capturing millions of hours of diverse, high-quality data. From first-person human demonstrations to bimanual leader-follower systems, we are teaching robots not just to move, but to understand.
Scale is building the tools to solve humanity's most stubborn challenges. Learn how we're laying the foundation for a new era of intelligence: https://scale.com/physical-ai

videos.title: Chain of Thought: Introducing SEAL Showdown
videos.description:
Brad Kenstler, Head of Agent Capabilities and Environments, discusses SEAL Showdown with Bing Liu, Head of Research, and Janie Gu, Product Manager. 

SEAL Showdown is a new public leaderboard – one built on real preferences, real people, and real-world use across practical categories like country, education level, age, language, and profession.

0:00 Introduction to Showdown
2:17 What makes Showdown unique
5:30 Showdown statistics and methodology
15:50 Preventing gaming and overfitting
18:20 Transparency and protecting user privacy
20:12 Dataset details
24:30 Surprising insights
28:44 Implications of Showdown to the research community
31:12 Future of the model evaluation landscape
36:38 Showdown's roadmap

Check out SEAL Showdown here: https://scale.com/showdown
